questions.question: What is the main purpose of the Program Counter (PC) in a CPU?
options: To store the address of the next instruction to be fetched.
To hold intermediate results of arithmetic operations.
To manage the flow of data between the CPU and main memory.
To register the current operational mode of the processor.
To cache recently used data values.

questions.question: In the digital IC design flow, after logic synthesis, what is the primary goal of the "Place" stage during physical implementation?
options: To convert the Register-Transfer Level (RTL) description into a gate-level netlist.
To optimize the power delivery network for reduced voltage drop.
To assign physical coordinates to all standard cells, macros, and I/O pins within the defined core area.
To verify the functional correctness of the design against specifications.
To route all the interconnects between placed components.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB) in a computer system with virtual memory?
options: To cache frequently accessed data blocks from main memory.
To provide a high-speed cache for virtual-to-physical address translations.
To manage the allocation and deallocation of physical memory pages.
To store the instruction set architecture (ISA) definitions.
To facilitate communication between multiple processing cores.

questions.question: In deep sub-micron technology nodes, which of the following post-layout parasitic effects has a significant impact on circuit timing and signal integrity, often requiring extensive analysis and mitigation strategies?
options: Alpha particle induced soft errors.
Thermal runaway in power transistors.
Electromigration in power traces.
Interconnect resistance-capacitance (RC) delay.
Quantum tunneling across gate oxides.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of performing Static Timing Analysis (STA) in the digital IC design flow?
options: To ensure the chip operates correctly at its specified clock frequency by verifying all timing paths meet setup and hold requirements.
To generate the final manufacturing mask data (GDSII).
To estimate the total silicon area required for the design.
To automatically insert test structures for design-for-testability (DFT).
To simulate the functional behavior of the circuit with actual input patterns.
